name: Traditional Christmas Pudding with Brandy Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a 2 pint pudding basin and use a large saucepan to hold the basin.
2. Wash currants, sultanas, and raisins in warm water and pat dry. Place fruit in a large bowl with candied peel and almonds.
3. Sift wholewheat flour, salt, and spices into the bowl with the fruit mixture. Add brown sugar, breadcrumbs, and vegetable suet. Mix well.
4. Stir in lemon juice, rind, and molasses with enough of the water and rum mixture to create a soft dough.
5. Transfer the mixture into the basin, cover with waxed paper and aluminum foil, and place the basin into a pot.
6. Pour enough water into the pot to reach halfway up the side of the basin. Bring to a boil, cover the saucepan, and let the pudding steam gently for 4 hours. Monitor the water level and add boiling water as needed.
7. When cooked, cool the pudding and store it in a cool, dry place for up to 2 months.
8. Before serving, steam the pudding again for 3 hours. Turn out onto a serving platter and flame with brandy if desired. (Dividing the pudding into two smaller puddings is also an option; it keeps up to 3 months in the refrigerator.)
